Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Return of the Obra Dinn: spherical mapped dithering for a 1bpp first-person game 271 upvotes, 39 comments
  2. Noq: n0's new QUIC implementation in Rust 166 upvotes, 23 comments
  3. Full Disclosure: A Third (and Fourth) Azure Sign-In Log Bypass Found 55 upvotes, 6 comments
  4. NanoGPT Slowrun: 10x Data Efficiency with Infinite Compute 113 upvotes, 22 comments
  5. From Oscilloscope to Wireshark: A UDP Story (2022) 91 upvotes, 18 comments
  6. My Random Forest Was Mostly Learning Time-to-Expiry Noise 28 upvotes, 3 comments
  7. Linux Page Faults, MMAP, and userfaultfd for faster VM boots 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  8. OpenBSD: PF queues break the 4 Gbps barrier 188 upvotes, 59 comments
  9. Show HN: Three new Kitten TTS models – smallest less than 25MB 358 upvotes, 138 comments
  10. Be intentional about how AI changes your codebase 86 upvotes, 29 comments
  11. Launch HN: Canary (YC W26) – AI QA that understands your code 45 upvotes, 14 comments
  12. Launch HN: Voltair (YC W26) – Drone and charging network for power utilities 63 upvotes, 22 comments
  13. Drugwars for the TI-82/83/83 Calculators 85 upvotes, 34 comments
  14. Scaling Karpathy's Autoresearch: What Happens When the Agent Gets a GPU Cluster 135 upvotes, 62 comments
  15. How the Turner twins are mythbusting modern technical apparel 154 upvotes, 78 comments
  16. Astral to Join OpenAI 1279 upvotes, 784 comments
  17. Push events into a running session with channels 232 upvotes, 128 comments
  18. “Your frustration is the product” 482 upvotes, 292 comments
  19. EsoLang-Bench: Evaluating Genuine Reasoning in LLMs via Esoteric Languages 76 upvotes, 37 comments
  20. Juggalo makeup blocks facial recognition technology (2019) 247 upvotes, 145 comments
  21. Cockpit is a web-based graphical interface for servers 199 upvotes, 120 comments
  22. Clockwise acquired by Salesforce and shutting down next week 96 upvotes, 55 comments
  23. An update on Steam / GOG changes for OpenTTD 288 upvotes, 198 comments
  24. Cover Flow with Modern CSS: Scroll-Driven Animations in Action (2025) 8 upvotes, 2 comments
  25. Waymo Safety Impact 257 upvotes, 249 comments
  26. Wayland set the Linux Desktop back by 10 years? 201 upvotes, 208 comments
  27. Google details new 24-hour process to sideload unverified Android apps 605 upvotes, 683 comments
  28. Last love: a romance in a care home (2023) 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  29. How many branches can your CPU predict? 42 upvotes, 35 comments
  30. 4Chan mocks £520k fine for UK online safety breaches 299 upvotes, 502 comments

Last generated 12:30pm, 20 March 2026. Source here.
